title: Precision@k Is a Great Target and a Dangerous Judge: Model Selection for Fixed-Budget Top-k Problems
description: Across 93 imbalanced datasets (90 real from imbalanced-learn and OpenML, 3 synthetic) and three model families (gradient boosting, random forests, logistic regression), we show that selecting a model by validation average precision yields significantly better held-out precision@k than selecting by precision@k itself (paired Wilcoxon p<1.2e-3 in every family) - optimizing selection for the exact target metric is a losing move (the winner's curse). Selection difficulty is governed not by a scale-free ratio (n_pos/K) but by the absolute count of true positives in the budget, whose model-free proxy min(K, n_pos) is knowable before training. Includes the full reproducible pipeline and the LaTeX preprint.
